title: sorbus umbellata var. orbiculata
intro: Sorbus umbellata var. orbiculata, also known as the mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America, characterized by its white or pink flowers and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Sorbus umbellata var. orbiculata include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, have been used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
